records.ety: [OE. suffren, soffren, OF. sufrir, sofrir, F. souffrir, (assumed) LL. sofferire, for L. sufferre; sub under + ferre to bear, akin to E. bear. See Bear to support.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Suffered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Suffering.]

defs: To feel, or endure, with pain, annoyance, etc.; to submit to with distress or grief; to undergo; as, to suffer pain of body, or grief of mind.
To endure or undergo without sinking; to support; to sustain; to bear up under.
quotes: Our spirit and strength entire, Strongly to suffer and support our pains.

defs: To undergo; to be affected by; to sustain; to experience; as, most substances suffer a change when long exposed to air and moisture; to suffer loss or damage.
quotes: If your more ponderous and settled project May suffer alteration.

defs: To allow; to permit; not to forbid or hinder; to tolerate.
quotes: Thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.
I suffer them to enter and possess.

defs: To feel or undergo pain of body or mind; to bear what is inconvenient; as, we suffer from pain, sickness, or sorrow; we suffer with anxiety.
quotes: O well for him whose will is strong! He suffers, but he will not suffer long.

defs: To undergo punishment; specifically, to undergo the penalty of death.
quotes: The father was first condemned to suffer upon a day appointed, and the son afterwards the day following.

defs: To be injured; to sustain loss or damage.
quotes: Public business suffers by private infirmities.
